plan.visual_reading: The arc opens on the Psalm's final illuminated line and travels a thousand years to the cross's finished word, holding the two as bookends of one redemption joined only by light — never collapsing the Hebrew 'done' and the Greek 'finished' into a verbal pun, but letting both rest on the same note of a completed saving work. From the scroll and the scribe telling it forward to a people not yet born, to the bowed head and the torn veil, to the day's end and the hands at rest, the eye moves toward a single gospel pivot: Christ on the cross saying it is done. It lands on the released burden, the open lit door, and the cross at dawn over the road home — nothing left to finish, only Someone to come home to.
plan.red_team_notes: Jaded Viewer risks: (1) an abstract 'finished work' theme can drift into floating-text slideshow — anchored every beat in a concrete tableau (frayed scroll thread, torn veil threads, set-down burden, lit doorway). (2) The resonance could be mis-read as a Hebrew/Greek pun — the diptych keeps the two scripts visibly DIFFERENT, joined only by a band of light, never overlaid. (3) Too many cross close-ups would feel repetitive — varied them (low-angle whole, close bowed head, distant silhouette trio, dawn-and-road) and spread non-cross anchors between them. Revision: removed the banned token 'frame' from scenes 1/3/5 subject_blocks (SP-G5).

scenes.title: The Psalm's Last Line
scenes.purpose: open the arc on the thread itself — the psalm's closing line caught in light
scenes.rationale: the hook is the anchor object, not a generic glowing book; the whole composition is weighted to one illuminated closing line
scenes.visible_elements: an ancient Hebrew scroll, its final lower line lit while the upper lines sit in shadow, frayed thread on the wooden stave
scenes.emotional_tone: an ending that is also a promise
scenes.subject_block: an ancient Hebrew scroll spread across pale stone, its upper lines sunk in cold shadow and its final lower line caught in a warm break of light, the parchment fibrous and worn, the ink old and cracked at the edges, a single frayed thread hanging from the rolled wooden stave, deep shadow beyond, the whole composition weighted toward that one illuminated closing line
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of an ending that is also a promise
macro_elements: the final line of Hebrew script in light
the frayed thread on the wooden stave

scenes.title: A People Not Yet Born
scenes.purpose: carry Ps 22:31's forward look — the saving work told to generations not yet born
scenes.rationale: uses the verse's own clause rather than a generic prophet at a desk
scenes.visible_elements: an aged scribe over the open psalm scroll by lamplight, dim background vignettes of future generations
scenes.emotional_tone: a promise told forward across centuries
scenes.subject_block: an aged scribe in deep blue and ochre robes seated low over the open psalm scroll at a stone table, his weathered face lifted in dawning hope, a clay lamp at the table's edge, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting a mother cradling an unborn promise, distant generations of faces in soft shadow, a far crowd at worship, and a child's open hand, the foreground scribe dominant and the vignettes half-dissolved memories of the people not yet born
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a promise carried forward across centuries
macro_elements: the scribe's hand at rest on the parchment
vignettes: a mother cradling an unborn promise

scenes.title: The Fingertip on the Final Word
scenes.purpose: rest on the psalm's last clause as it is quoted
scenes.rationale: a macro anchor for the quoted line, avoiding a wide reading-room cliche
scenes.visible_elements: a weathered fingertip beneath the last clause of Hebrew script, cracked dark ink, lamplit parchment
scenes.emotional_tone: a finished word held in stillness
scenes.subject_block: an extreme close view of the open parchment, a single weathered fingertip at rest beneath the last clause of ancient Hebrew script, the ink dark and cracked, the parchment lamplit and fibrous, deep shadow at the upper edge, the fingertip the still centre of the image
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a finished word held in stillness

scenes.title: It Is Finished
scenes.purpose: the gospel pivot — the finished saving work on the cross, the One who said it is done
scenes.rationale: the CTA's destination; designed iconic, near-still and reverent to bookend the cut, not the most emotional moment
scenes.visible_elements: the crucified Christ from a low angle, head bowed, robed at the loins, dark sky, a faint light behind the lowered head
scenes.subject_block: the crucified Christ seen from a low angle against a darkened sky, his head bowed low in the moment after the final word, the body robed at the loins, the face calm in completion, the arms outstretched along the dark beam, a faint break of pale light behind the lowered head, the bare hill beneath, a held and reverent tableau of a finished work
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a saving work completed
macro_elements: the pierced hand on the beam
the faint light behind the head
the bare hill beneath the cross

scenes.title: The Greek Word
scenes.purpose: John's finished word on the page — a thousand years and a different tongue from the psalm
scenes.rationale: shows the visibly DIFFERENT script, guarding the resonance-not-citation truth
scenes.visible_elements: an early Greek codex page, a line of Greek letters, a fingertip beneath, raking lamplight
scenes.subject_block: an extreme close view of an early Greek codex page, a single line of dark Greek letters on pale vellum, a weathered fingertip at rest beneath the line, warm lamplight raking across the surface, the ink crisp, deep shadow at the page's outer edge, the lettering the still focus of the image

scenes.title: A Thousand Years Apart
scenes.purpose: hold scroll and cross as two bookends of one redemption, joined only by light
scenes.rationale: states the resonance as composition — never an overlay or a pun
scenes.visible_elements: the scribe over his scroll at left, a distant cross on a dark hill at right, a vertical band of pale light between
scenes.subject_block: a wide composition divided by a vertical band of pale light, at the left the aged scribe low over his psalm scroll by lamplight with his face lifting, at the right across the divide a distant cross on a dark hill under a breaking sky, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting a setting sun, an open road, rolling generations, and a far temple, the two halves joined only by the band of light between them
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of two bookends of one redemption

scenes.title: The Bowed Head
scenes.purpose: the peace of completion in the lowered face
scenes.rationale: a close read of the finished moment without repeating the whole-cross framing
scenes.visible_elements: the crucified Christ's lowered head and shoulder, crown of thorns, faint light along the brow
scenes.subject_block: a close reverent view of the crucified Christ's lowered head and shoulder, the face turned down in the peace of completion, a crown of thorns resting upon the brow, a faint light along the temple, the dark grain of the beam behind, soft shadow swallowing the edges, the stillness of a finished thing
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of peace after the finished cry
macro_elements: the faint light on the brow
the dark grain of the beam

scenes.title: The Torn Veil
scenes.purpose: the finished work opens the way — the temple veil rent (Matt 27:51)
scenes.rationale: a real NT consequence of the finished work, not an invented symbol
scenes.visible_elements: the temple veil rent top to bottom, golden light through the tear, dust caught in the shaft
scenes.emotional_tone: a way opened by a finished work
scenes.subject_block: the great temple veil rent from top to bottom, the heavy embroidered cloth split down its centre and fallen open, dim golden light spilling through the new opening from the holy place beyond, dust caught mid-air in the shaft of light, the torn threads frayed along the tear, a frozen tableau of a barrier finished
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a way opened by a finished work
macro_elements: the torn centre of the veil

scenes.title: The Day Is Done
scenes.purpose: the held quiet of a day, and a work, brought to its end
scenes.rationale: a breathing wide beat that says 'it is done' without another face
scenes.visible_elements: three bare crosses in silhouette on a dark hill, a low red sun at the horizon
scenes.emotional_tone: a day brought to its close
scenes.subject_block: a wide view of three bare crosses in silhouette on a darkened hill, the sun low and red at the horizon behind them, the sky deep with the last light of day, the ground sunk in shadow, no figures near, the held quiet of a day come to its end
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a day, and a work, brought to its close
macro_elements: the last band of horizon light

scenes.title: Hands at Rest
scenes.purpose: labour ended — the pierced hands utterly still
scenes.rationale: the finished work read in the body, not in text
scenes.visible_elements: two open pierced palms at rest, nail-marks dark in the centres, garment edge beneath
scenes.subject_block: a close view of two weathered pierced hands lying open and at rest, palms turned upward, the healed nail-marks dark in the centres, a rough garment edge beneath them, warm light across the open palms against deep shadow, the hands utterly still, labour ended
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of finished labour and rest
macro_elements: the open palm with its nail-mark
the warm light across the palms

scenes.title: The Finished Work
scenes.purpose: the central cross holding everything the finished work secures
scenes.rationale: the theological centre as one coherent image with half-dissolved echoes, not a montage
scenes.visible_elements: the central cross with the robed figure, dim vignettes of scroll, torn veil, open door, generations
scenes.subject_block: the cross at the centre against a breaking sky, the robed crucified figure calm in completion with head bowed, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting the open psalm scroll, the torn temple veil, an open door with light beyond, and distant generations coming forward, the central cross dominant and the vignettes half-dissolved echoes of all the finished work secures
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of one completed work holding everything
macro_elements: the breaking sky behind the beam
the faint glow at the cross's foot
vignettes: an open door with light beyond

scenes.title: Nothing Left to Carry
scenes.purpose: the conviction — the weight laid down, nothing left to finish
scenes.rationale: embodies 'nothing left for you' without a debt/transaction framing
scenes.visible_elements: a kneeling figure at the foot of a rough cross, a heavy burden set down beside loosened hands
scenes.subject_block: a kneeling figure at the foot of a rough cross, a heavy travelling burden set down on the ground beside loosened open hands, the shoulders eased, the head bowed in relief, warm light falling on the released load and the bare feet, deep shadow beyond, the weight laid down with nothing more to bear
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a weight finally laid down
macro_elements: the rough foot of the cross

scenes.title: The Open Door Home
scenes.purpose: the landing's invitation — Someone to come home to
scenes.rationale: an open-home image rather than a literal altar-call cliche
scenes.visible_elements: a humble doorway standing open, warm lamplight across the threshold, a worn path, an olive tree
scenes.emotional_tone: a home left open and waiting
scenes.subject_block: a humble doorway standing open at the end of a worn path, warm golden lamplight spilling out across the threshold into the blue dusk, a single olive tree beside the door, the path leading the eye inward, no figure yet upon it, the held invitation of a home left open and lit
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a home left open and waiting
macro_elements: the warm light across the threshold
the olive tree beside the door

scenes.title: The One Who Said It Is Done
scenes.purpose: close on the bare cross at dawn — the finished work, quiet and iconic
scenes.rationale: lands the cut on a clean, iconic empty cross at dawn; no figure, no road, no door — nothing competes
scenes.visible_elements: a bare wooden cross at dawn, warm gold light breaking from behind the upright beam, deep dawn sky, no figures
scenes.emotional_tone: a finished work and a breaking dawn
scenes.subject_block: a bare wooden cross standing empty against a widening dawn, warm gold light breaking from directly behind the upright beam and rimming its edges, the grain and joints of the rough timber catching the light, a deep dawn sky of amber and blue beyond, the hill's crest in soft shadow at the base, no figures and no other structures, the empty cross alone and iconic at the centre
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a finished work and a breaking dawn
macro_elements: the empty crossbeam against the dawn
the gold light breaking behind the beam
the grain and joints of the rough timber

plan.rationale: Same 14-scene set, with the banned token 'frame' removed from the subject_blocks of scenes 1, 3, and 5 (now 'composition' / 'the still centre of the image' / 'the still focus of the image'). The resonance stays honest (different scripts joined only by light); ten singles anchor the literal beats, four unified scenes carry the theology and echoes; hero #4 'It Is Finished' is the gospel pivot that bookends the cut. Rejected alternatives unchanged: no Hebrew/Greek overlay pun, no 'paid in full' ledger, no resurrection finish (this short's pivot is the finished work on the cross).

panel.note: The turn is carried, not merely illustrated: hook [1] 'The Psalm's Last Line' -> proof [4] 'It Is Finished' -> conviction [6] 'A Thousand Years Apart' -> landing [13]/[14] 'The Open Door Home'/'The One Who Said It Is Done'. The thread (two bookends of one finished work) drives the arc.

Resonance-not-citation honoured: [5] 'The Greek Word' and [3] 'The Fingertip on the Final Word' keep Hebrew and Greek visibly distinct (no pun/overlay); [8] 'The Torn Veil' is the real Matt 27:51 consequence; no debt/'paid in full' frame; no resurrection over-reach (pivot is the finished work on the cross).
[9] 'The Day Is Done' (three crosses, red sun) is the nearest-to-stock beat; defensible as a breathing wide hold but render it with real Baroque weight so it doesn't read as a worship slide.
jesus_variant 'passion' consistent across [4][6][7][10][11][14]; identical crucified/robed-at-the-loins description, no drift.

Banned token 'frame' now removed from [1][3][5] subject_blocks ('composition' / 'the still centre of the image' / 'the still focus of the image'); prose is state-only, 14 scenes within the rich-passage window.
Scroll-and-cross is a known pairing, but [6] joining the two ONLY by a band of light and [2] 'A People Not Yet Born' as a forward-looking vignette deliver an honest, non-predictable payoff.

gates.evidence: Ps 22:31 scroll/scribe, John 19:30 cross, Matt 27:51 torn veil — all defensible; no invented narrative; no unwarranted halos.
Every beat id in ['hook', 'point', 'proof', 'conviction', 'landing'] has >=1 supporting scene.
10 single + 4 unified; anchors vary widely; no unrationalised cliche.
Resonance stated as resonance; different scripts joined by light; no foreign doctrine.
All subject_block + mood_block content clear of the banned-token list.
jesus_variant 'passion' consistent; identical canonical crucified description.

panel.note: Independent read: the arc genuinely turns — [1] scroll's last line opens the thread, [4] 'It Is Finished' is the proof pivot, [6] 'A Thousand Years Apart' holds the conviction, [13]/[14] land the invitation. Not a slideshow.

The load-bearing guard survives scrutiny: [5] 'The Greek Word' and [3] Hebrew fingertip are never overlaid, so no false etymological citation; [8] torn veil is Matt 27:51, real. Pivot is the cross's finished work, correctly NOT the resurrection.
[9] 'The Day Is Done' is the most familiar image; acceptable as a single breathing wide beat among 13 fresher anchors (codex, torn veil, set-down burden, lit door).
All Jesus-bearing scenes are jesus_variant 'passion', crucified/robed, consistent face and wardrobe; no drift across [4][6][7][10][11][14].
Subject_blocks are state-only and banned-token clean after the revision; 14 scenes appropriate for the rich pairing; each scene earns its slot.
Would not predict the diptych-joined-only-by-light [6] or the 'people not yet born' vignette [2]; honest surprise, not contrarian.

gates.evidence: All literal scenes defensible against Ps 22:31 / John 19:30 / Matt 27:51; no invented detail.
Every beat id in ['hook', 'point', 'proof', 'conviction', 'landing'] has >=1 supporting scene.
All subject_block + mood_block content clear of the banned-token list.
